channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 136
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 136: world daily loss $81.26B, cumulative world loss $8.62T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 135
item.description: 2026-07-12 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 134
item.description: Day 134 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.76B/day, world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 133
item.description: SEO update for 2026-07-10: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 132
item.description: Internet blackout day 132: modeled daily loss reaches $81.26B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 131
item.description: 2026-07-08 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 130
item.description: Day 130 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 129
item.description: 2026-07-06 internet blackout simulation: day 129, world scenario $81.26B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 128
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 128: world daily loss $81.26B, cumulative world loss $7.97T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 127
item.description: 2026-07-04 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 126
item.description: Day 126 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.76B/day, world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 125
item.description: SEO update for 2026-07-02: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 124
item.description: Internet blackout day 124: modeled daily loss reaches $81.26B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 123
item.description: 2026-06-30 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 122
item.description: Day 122 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 121
item.description: 2026-06-28 internet blackout simulation: day 121, world scenario $81.26B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 120
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 120: world daily loss $81.26B, cumulative world loss $7.32T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 119
item.description: 2026-06-26 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 118
item.description: Day 118 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.76B/day, world $81.26B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 117
item.description: SEO update for 2026-06-24: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $81.26B/day.
